channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Criticism of Starmer’s Foreign Policy and Impact on UK’s Global Standing
item.description: In this column, Richard Littlejohn sharply criticises Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s handling of the UK's role in international military affairs, particularly regarding the United States and Iran. Litt...

item.title: Middle East tensions threaten schedules of major international sports events
item.description: The escalating conflict between Iran and the United States is beginning to have a tangible impact on the global sporting calendar, creating uncertainty for several high-profile athletes and events. Fo...

item.title: United Airlines Flight Makes Safe Emergency Landing at LAX Following Engine Issue
item.description: On Monday morning, a United Airlines Boeing 787-9 flight bound for New Jersey had to make an emergency return to Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) after reports of a potential engine fire. Fligh...

item.title: US Company Acquires UK Brewdog Operations, Leading to Bar Closures and Job Losses
item.description: Brewdog, the Scottish craft beer brand founded in 2007, has entered administration after struggling with losses and mounting debt in recent years. US-based Tilray, a company involved in beverages and ...

item.title: Starmer defends UK decision on Iran strikes amid US-Israeli action
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer has addressed Parliament to clarify the UK's stance following US and Israeli strikes on Iran. He explained that the government does not support 'regime change from the skies' and chos...

item.title: Oil and Gas Prices Rise Amid Middle East Conflict While Certain Stocks Show Resilience
item.description: Oil and gas prices experienced sharp increases as markets reacted to recent conflict in the Middle East. Brent crude initially jumped by 13% to $82 per barrel during early Asian trading before settlin...

item.title: X introduces official Paid Partnership labels for creator posts
item.description: Social media platform X has introduced a new 'Paid Partnership' label to help creators identify sponsored content on their posts. Previously, creators had to rely on hashtags like #ad or #paidpartners...

item.title: The Decline of Taxonomy: Art Borkent and the Future of Species Identification
item.description: Art Borkent, a 72-year-old insect taxonomist, has described over 300 species of biting midges, a family of flies known for their diverse feeding habits, including blood-sucking from mammals, reptiles,...

item.title: Ramses II exhibition at Battersea Power Station reconsiders the legacy of Egypt’s most self-promoting pharaoh
item.description: A major exhibition, “Ramses and the Pharaohs’ Gold”, arrives at Battersea Power Station, offering London audiences a rare opportunity to explore treasures associated with Ramses II, one of ancient Egy...

item.title: Dalmia-Gopichand Badminton Academy Designed as a Shuttlecock-Inspired Sports Facility
item.description: At the edge of Kalinga Stadium in Bhubaneswar, the Dalmia-Gopichand Badminton Academy, also called 'The Shuttle,' introduces an innovative architectural design that merges functionality with visual im...

item.title: Key global developments: Iran succession, Pakistan unrest, Ukraine drone strikes and environmental concerns
item.description: Today's global briefing highlights a series of significant international developments. In Iran, attention is focused on Khomeini’s grandson as a potential successor to the country’s Supreme Leader ami...

item.title: Study Finds Accelerated Biological Aging and Cognitive Decline in Young Cancer Survivors
item.description: Teens and young adults who survive cancer may experience accelerated biological aging and early cognitive decline compared with their peers, according to new research published in Nature Communication...

item.title: Wife of Iran’s Supreme Leader Dies Following US-Israeli Airstrike as Regional Conflict Escalates
item.description: Mansoureh Khojasteh Bagherzadeh, the 79-year-old wife of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has died from injuries sustained in a US-Israeli airstrike that also killed her husband at his co...

item.title: Subsurface Oceans on Icy Moons Could Experience Boiling Due to Tidal Heating
item.description: Recent research from the University of California - Davis suggests that icy moons in the outer solar system may be more geologically active than previously thought. Tidal forces from massive planets c...

item.title: Parts of England Record Wettest Winter Since 1836 as UK Rainfall Rises Above Average
item.description: Provisional figures from the Met Office show that parts of the United Kingdom have experienced their wettest winter since comparable records began in 1836. Cornwall, Leicestershire and the West Midlan...

item.title: NYU Researchers Control Crystal Formation Using Light-Sensitive Molecules
item.description: Researchers at New York University have developed a method to precisely control the formation and behavior of microscopic crystals using light. By introducing light-sensitive molecules called photoaci...

item.title: Regional tensions rise as conflict between US, Israel, and Iran intensifies
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East has intensified following US and Israeli attacks on Iran after the death of Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. President Donald Trump warned that the worst ...

item.title: Strikes Between the US and Iran Disrupt Oil Tanker Traffic in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has slowed dramatically following escalating military strikes between the United States and Iran, according to an analysis by Sky News’ Data and Forensics...

item.title: James Vowles outlines Williams’ long-term rebuilding plan and Formula One title ambitions
item.description: James Vowles, the Williams team principal, reflects on three years in charge as he attempts to guide the historic Formula One team back to the front of the grid. Having left Mercedes in 2022 after a p...

item.title: Ms Rachel Returns with a Groundbreaking Children's Special Promoting Inclusivity and Friendship
item.description: After a four-month hiatus, Rachel Accurso, known as Ms Rachel, has released her latest children’s programme, 'Learn With Ms Rachel – Friendship & Social Skills', which has been hailed as a remarkable ...
